summary: R2 fixes the QA blocker by giving the M2 economy/population property invariant a narrow explicit 15s test timeout while preserving its workload, seed, input ranges, and assertions. The default checked-in `pnpm --filter @monsoon/sim-core test` command now passes without reviewer-supplied timeout flags.

tests_run.result: Clean after R2 implementation commit effaa013429805bffc597e58718e30aed75ae7ab.
Content validation passed; M2 prototype fixture remained districts=14 settlements=6 routes=25 mapGeometries=20 manifestHash=d474a105.
26 test files passed, 198 tests passed under the checked-in package command with no added CLI timeout.
format:check, lint, typecheck, and architecture:check passed; architecture self-test produced 15 expected violations.

decisions.decision: Kept the property invariant workload unchanged at numRuns=24, seed=1531, days 0-420, group ids 1-14, and labor 1-80; added a 15s timeout only to this test because advancing 24 generated worlds can exceed Vitest's 5s default in this workspace.
Did not change topology fixture content, compiler/runtime/sim-core authority code, client/rendering paths, M7 content-lock conclusions, or downstream topology-client scope.

risks.summary: The invariant still performs full generated world advancement, so it remains a relatively expensive sim-core test. R2 stabilizes the default command without reducing coverage.

requested_action: Re-review M7-MAP-TOPOLOGY-FIXTURE-001 R2, focusing on the default sim-core package test now passing without manual timeout flags.
